children.heading: Immunity for reports of suspected terrorist activity or suspicious behavior
children.label: a Immunity for reports of suspected terrorist activity or suspicious behavior

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any person who, in good faith and based on objectively reasonable suspicion, makes, or causes to be made, a voluntary report of covered activity to an authorized official shall be immune from civil liability under Federal, State, and local law for such report.

children.heading: False reports
children.label: 2 False reports
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any report that the person knew to be false or was made with reckless disregard for the truth at the time that person made that report.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any authorized official who observes, or receives a report of, covered activity and takes reasonable action in good faith to respond to such activity shall have qualified immunity from civil liability for such action, consistent with applicable law in the relevant jurisdiction. An authorized official as defined by subsection (d)(1)(A) not entitled to assert the defense of qualified immunity shall nevertheless be immune from civil liability under Federal, State, and local law if such authorized official takes reasonable action, in good faith, to respond to the reported activity.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: 2 Savings clause
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall affect the ability of any authorized official to assert any defense, privilege, or immunity that would otherwise be available, and this subsection shall not be construed as affecting any such defense, privilege, or immunity.

children.heading: Attorney fees and costs
children.label: c Attorney fees and costs
children.text: Any person or authorized official found to be immune from civil liability under this section shall be entitled to recover from the plaintiff all reasonable costs and attorney fees.

children.label: A
children.text: any employee or agent of a passenger transportation system or other person with responsibilities relating to the security of such systems;

children.label: B
children.text: any officer, employee, or agent of the Department of Homeland Security, the Department of Transportation, or the Department of Justice with responsibilities relating to the security of passenger transportation systems; or

children.label: C
children.text: any Federal, State, or local law enforcement officer.

children.label: A
children.text: a threat to a passenger transportation system or passenger safety or security; or

children.label: B
children.text: an act of terrorism (as that term is defined in ).section 3077 of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: public transportation, as defined in ;section 5302 of title 49

children.label: B
children.text: over-the-road bus transportation, as defined in subchapter IV, and school bus transportation;

children.label: C
children.text: intercity passenger rail transportation as defined in ;12section 24102 of title 49So in original. Probably should be “intercity rail passenger”.1So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.2

children.label: D
children.text: the transportation of passengers onboard a passenger vessel as defined in ;2section 2101 of title 46

children.label: E
children.text: other regularly scheduled waterborne transportation service of passengers by vessel of at least 20 gross tons; and

children.label: F
children.text: air transportation, as defined in , of passengers.section 40102 of title 49

children.heading: Passenger transportation system
children.label: 4 Passenger transportation system
children.text: The term “passenger transportation system” means an entity or entities organized to provide passenger transportation using vehicles, including the infrastructure used to provide such transportation.

children.heading: Vehicle
children.label: 5 Vehicle
children.text: The term “vehicle” has the meaning given to that term in section 1992(16) of title 18.3So in original. Probably should be section “1992(d)(16)”.3

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: e Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect on , and shall apply to all activities and claims occurring on or after such date.October 1, 2006
